2604.25684.title: Think Before You Act -- A Neurocognitive Governance Model for Autonomous AI Agents
2604.25684.paper_summary: The rapid deployment of autonomous AI agents across enterprise, healthcare, and safety-critical environments has created a fundamental governance gap. Existing approaches, runtime guardrails, training-time alignment, and post-hoc auditing treat governance as an external constraint rather than an internalized behavioral principle, leaving agents vulnerable to unsafe and irreversible actions. We address this gap by drawing on how humans self-govern naturally: before acting, humans engage deliberate cognitive processes grounded in executive function, inhibitory control, and internalized organizational rules to evaluate whether an intended action is permissible, requires modification, or demands escalation. This paper proposes a neurocognitive governance framework that formally maps this human self-governance process to LLM-driven agent reasoning, establishing a structural parallel between the human brain and the large language model as the cognitive core of an agent. We formalize a Pre-Action Governance Reasoning Loop (PAGRL) in which agents consult a four-layer governance rule set: global, workflow-specific, agent-specific, and situational before every consequential action, mirroring how human organizations structure compliance hierarchies across enterprise, department, and role levels. Implemented on a production-grade retail supply chain workflow, the framework achieves 95% compliance accuracy and zero false escalations to human oversight, demonstrating that embedding governance into agent reasoning produces more consistent, explainable, and auditable compliance than external enforcement. This work offers a principled foundation for autonomous AI agents that govern themselves the way humans do: not because rules are imposed upon them, but because deliberation is embedded in how they think.
2604.25684.paper_summary_zh: 自主 AI 代理在企業、醫療保健和安全關鍵環境中的快速部署創造了一個根本性的治理缺口。現有的方法、運行時護欄、訓練時對齊和事後審計將治理視為外部約束，而非內化的行為原則，這使得代理容易受到不安全和不可逆行為的影響。我們通過借鑒人類自然自我治理的方式來解決這一缺口：在行動之前，人類會進行基於執行功能、抑制控制和內化組織規則的深思熟慮的認知過程，以評估預期行動是否可行、是否需要修改或是否需要升級。本文提出了一個神經認知治理框架，正式將這一人類自我治理過程映射到基於大型語言模型的代理推理中，建立了人腦與大型語言模型之間的結構性平行關係，作為代理的認知核心。我們形式化了一個前行動治理推理循環（PAGRL），在每次重要行動之前，代理會參考一套四層治理規則：全球、工作流程特定、代理特定和情境，這反映了人類組織如何在企業、部門和角色層級上結構化合規層級。該框架在生產級零售供應鏈工作流程中實施，實現了 95% 的合規準確率和零錯誤升級至人類監督，證明將治理嵌入代理推理中比外部執法產生更一致、可解釋和可審計的合規性。這項工作為自主 AI 代理提供了一個原則性基礎，使其以人類的方式自我治理：不是因為規則被強加於他們，而是因為深思熟慮嵌入了他們的思維方式。
2604.25684.author: Eranga Bandara et.al.
2604.25684.authors: Eranga Bandara, Ross Gore, Asanga Gunaratna, Sachini Rajapakse, Isurunima Kularathna, Ravi Mukkamala, Sachin Shetty, Xueping Liang, Amin Hass, Tharaka Hewa, Abdul Rahman, Christopher K. Rhea, Anita H. Clayton, Preston Samuel, Atmaram Yarlagadda
